mission.title: kubevela: [Feature] Workflow need support new step type to  batch apply a lot of components parallelly or in order
mission.description: [Feature] Workflow need support new step type to  batch apply a lot of components parallelly or in order. Requested by 12+ users.

steps.title: Check current kubevela deployment
steps.description:
Verify your kubevela version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n kubevela -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kubevela
helm list -n kubevela 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working kubevela installation.

steps.title: Review kubevela configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant kubevela configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n kubevela -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kubevela
kubectl get configmap -n kubevela -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=kubevela
```
**Request description**
For example, I have 6 components in a OAM application，the first and second one need apply before the last four components and wait user to confirm after it apply success.

steps.title: Apply the fix for [Feature] Workflow need support new step type to  batch…
steps.description:
> After talked with  in community meeting. We found that this is really good scenario for deploy application into clusters in high availability mod. We can use sub-steps to deploy on component to different clusters in parallel.
> 
> I propose the solution can be like this:
> 
> ```
> workflow:
>  
```yaml
workflow:
  steps:
  - type: group
    name: my-parent-step
    properties:
       ...
    subSteps:
     - type: deploy
       name: deploy-comp-1
       properties:
           ...
```

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n kubevela -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kubevela
kubectl get events -n kubevela --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "[Feature] Workflow need support new step type to  batch…" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
> After talked with  in community meeting. We found that this is really good scenario for deploy application into clusters in high availability mod. We can use sub-steps to deploy on component to different clusters in parallel.
> 
> I propose the solution can be like this:
> 
> ```
> workflow:
>   steps:
>   - type: group
>     name: my-parent-step
>     properties:
>        ...
codeSnippets:
workflow:
  steps:
  - type: group
    name: my-parent-step
    properties:
       ...
    subSteps:
     - type: deploy
       name: deploy-comp-1
       properties:
           ...

status:
  workflow:
    steps:
    - type: group
      name: my-parent-step
      message: ...
      subSteps:
      - type:  sub-step1
        name: deploy-comp-1
        message: ...

workflow:
  steps:
  - name: my-parent-step
    subSteps:
     - type: deploy
       name: deploy-comp-1
       properties:
           ...

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with kubevela installed or the issue environment reproducible.
